facilizeCase.title: Facilize
facilizeCase.subtitle: A complete SaaS platform that centralizes scheduling, finances, inventory and CRM — from solo professionals to multi-branch networks.

productContext.title: The Problem
productContext.problem: Service providers waste time and money with spreadsheets, notebooks, and disconnected systems.
productContext.audience: The target audience ranges from solo beauty and health professionals to networks with multiple branches and dozens of employees.
productContext.purpose: Facilize unifies online scheduling, financial control, inventory, CRM, and team management in a single platform — with Stripe, Google Calendar, and real-time notifications.
productContext.differential: Modular multi-tenant architecture where every completed appointment automatically triggers financial transactions, inventory deduction, commission calculation, and CRM updates — with zero manual intervention.

modules.title: Platform
modules.intro: Modules covering the full business cycle

items.title: Scheduling
items.description: Real-time coordination layer with specialized services for conflict validation, per-employee availability, time blocks, approvals, notifications, and automatic integration with finances, inventory, and commissions.

items.title: Services
items.description: Catalog with flexible pricing: fixed, hourly, or quote-based. Cost calculation with ingredients, profit margin, and per-employee assignment. Presential, online, or home visit modalities.

items.title: Finances
items.description: Encrypted transactions, multiple accounts, credit cards with automatic invoices, recurring reminders, savings goals, and fiscal note emission.

items.title: CRM
items.description: Organizational customers, notes, interactions, and full history. Invoices, per-customer revenue metrics, and automatic follow-ups.

items.title: ERP & Team
items.description: Multi-branch, 100+ granular permissions, access levels, commissions (%, fixed, or hybrid), sales goals, ranking, and individual cash registers.

items.title: Inventory
items.description: Products with automatic movements linked to appointments. Per-branch inventory with automatic deduction and reversal.

items.title: Reports
items.description: PDF, CSV, and Excel with templates. Scheduling, financial, inventory, commissions, and performance analytics.

items.title: Integrations
items.description: Payment-provider webhooks for subscription lifecycle, Google Calendar OAuth, WebSocket for real-time notifications, and Prometheus-compatible observability.

userFlow.title: Flexible Architecture
userFlow.subtitle: From solo professionals to companies with dozens of employees

items.title: Solo Professional
items.description: Simplified dashboard, personal calendar, public booking link, and integrated financial control. Plans with volume-adjusted limits.

items.title: Company
items.description: Multi-branch with employee hierarchy, department permissions, chained approvals, team goals, and consolidated reports.

items.title: 100+ Granular Permissions
items.description: Authorization system by resource, profile, and company with 5 access levels: basic, departmental, managerial, executive, and admin.

items.title: Approval Flow
items.description: Receptionist books → manager approves. History of approvals, rejections, and action audit.

items.title: Automatic Side-effects
items.description: Completed appointment triggers financial transaction, inventory deduction, commission calculation, and CRM update automatically.

items.title: Observability stack
items.description: Prometheus-compatible metrics, dashboards for capacity and HTTP health, and runtime signals that support reliable production operations.

items.title: Financial Encryption
items.description: Sensitive transaction, account, and category data encrypted at rest with on-demand decryption.

items.title: Plans & Limits
items.description: Feature and limit control by plan: allowed appointment types, recurrence, home visits, and monthly quotas.

observability.label: Observability & operations
observability.title: Metrics, dashboards, and operational awareness
observability.lead: The backend integrates with a Prometheus-compatible metrics stack: time series feed internal dashboards and support capacity decisions. Cache and database layers expose health signals for operators. A scheduled evaluator correlates recent trends and notifies trusted contacts when patterns suggest abuse, instability, or incident conditions—with safeguards so gaps in the metrics path do not produce misleading notifications.
pipelineItems: Runtime and Node-level signals: memory footprint, CPU pressure, event-loop responsiveness, and related process health indicators.
HTTP-level aggregates for the API—throughput, error mix, and latency percentiles—alongside domain-oriented counters where they help triage production issues.
Degraded but safe behavior when the metrics backend is unavailable, avoiding automated actions on incomplete data.
infraItems: Cache tier: connectivity checks and coarse capacity indicators suitable for ops dashboards.
Primary datastore: connectivity health and connection-pressure indicators without exposing internal statistics commands or schemas.
observability.alertsLead: Policy-driven rules run on a regular cadence, classify findings by severity, and group related conditions so operators can respond without alert fatigue.
alertsBullets: Notifications go only to designated operational contacts through the platform mail channel; nothing is sent when no eligible recipient exists.
Repeated alerts of the same class are throttled so sustained incidents do not flood inboxes.

Coverage is described at architecture level—authentication stress, server-side error rates, latency regression, unusual traffic, persistence connectivity—without publishing thresholds, environment keys, metric names, or implementation identifiers that could aid abuse.
observability.configClosing: Tuning, routing, credentials, and rollout details live in private configuration and change management, not in public copy, so this case study communicates capability and architecture without a playbook for bypassing safeguards.

fullstack.label: My Role
fullstack.title: End-to-end development, from product to infrastructure
bullets: Product ideation, business model, and roadmap
Modular architecture with automatic cross-module integration
Design system, dashboards, employee portal, and public pages
REST APIs with business validations and granular authorization
Multi-tenant modeling with Prisma and versioned migrations
Deployment with Docker, CI/CD, and AWS infrastructure

frontend.title: Frontend
frontend.intro: Three distinct portals — owner, employee, and public — each with flows optimized for their use context.
bullets: Design system with tokens, variables, and reusable components
Complex state handling: loading, error, empty, and success states
Full responsiveness, including dashboards and data tables
Lazy loading, code splitting, and image optimization
API consumption with retry, caching, and error handling
Authentication with JWT, 2FA, and Google OAuth

architecture.title: Components
architecture.content: Atomic composition with custom hooks. UI separated from business logic.

responsiveness.title: Responsiveness
responsiveness.content: Mobile-first with horizontal-scroll tables and adaptive layouts.

performance.title: Performance
performance.content: Per-route code splitting, lazy module loading, and data caching.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.content: JWT, 2FA, OAuth, and session management with device control.

backend.title: Backend
backend.intro: Modular NestJS API with 15+ specialized services per domain, each encapsulating its rules and integrating automatically via events and side-effects.
bullets: Modular architecture across Auth, Scheduling, Finances, CRM, ERP, and Reports—each domain encapsulates rules, validation, and authorization
100+ granular permissions with 5 access levels and per-employee customization
Appointment approval flow with history and action audit
Automatic side-effects: appointment completion triggers finances, inventory, commissions, and CRM
Payment-provider webhooks, Google Calendar OAuth, and real-time WebSocket notifications
Audit log for all critical actions with filters and export

database.title: Database
database.content: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM. Multi-tenant modeling with complex cross-domain relations, versioned migrations, and sensitive data encryption.

cta.title: Explore Facilize
cta.subtitle: See the platform in action and explore the modules hands-on.

acompanhamentoCase.title: Clinical & sports follow-up platform
acompanhamentoCase.subtitle: Full-stack system for authorized teams to record progress, metrics, and formal reports — without exposing sensitive data on the public web.
acompanhamentoCase.context: Built for internal use: consolidate follow-up information over time, with distinct roles (admin, professional, reader) and authorization enforced on the API, not only in the UI.
acompanhamentoCase.architectureLead: The front end consumes a REST API over JSON on HTTPS; the API base URL is environment-driven so the client is not tied to a fixed host.

layers.title: UI (Next.js / React)
layers.description: App Router, forms, listings, and dashboard with emphasis on UX and accessibility — desktop sidebar layout, reusable components, and a centralized HTTP client for tokens and errors.

layers.title: API (NestJS)
layers.description: Domain modules, Prisma on PostgreSQL, JWT with Passport, DTO validation, and PDF (PDFKit) and spreadsheet (ExcelJS) generation when exports are required.

productBullets: Relational schema for people under care, episodes, rehabilitation phases, tests and metrics, and discharge reports with consistency rules.
Closure criteria implemented with context-specific strategies (Strategy pattern), avoiding a single monolithic block.
Dashboard with aggregated indicators to support team decisions.
Enums in the database for controlled types and states, reducing data drift.
acompanhamentoCase.securityBody: Authentication required for restricted areas, role guards on the server, and redirect to login when the API returns unauthorized. Sensitive data only over secure channels; operational detail and clinical content stay out of public materials.
